title: Seasons
description:
Implement a Seasons class that represents seasons and provides methods to work with them.

**Requirements:**
- Create a Season class with a constructor that takes a season name
- Implement `__str__()` to return a nice string representation
- Add methods to get information about the season
- Create an `__eq__()` method to compare seasons

**Example:**
```
spring = Season("Spring")
print(spring)  # Displays season information
```
check50_slug:
difficulty": "easy",
    "common_mistakes": [
      "Not implementing dunder methods (__str__, __eq__)",
      "Incorrect method signatures",
      "Not validating season names"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "student",
    "title": "Student",
    "description": "Implement a Student class to represent a student.

**Requirements:**
- Constructor takes name and house (like Gryffindor)
- Implement `__str__()` to return formatted string
- Implement `__repr__()` for debugging
- Add a class variable to track all students
- Add methods to update student information

**Example:**
```
s = Student("Harry", "Gryffindor")
print(s)  # Harry from Gryffindor
```",
    "check50_slug": "cs50/problems/2024/python/student",
    "difficulty": "easy",
    "common_mistakes": [
      "Not implementing __repr__()",
      "Incorrect attribute access in methods",
      "Not using self correctly"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "bank",
    "title": "Bank",
    "description": "Implement a BankAccount class with deposit and withdraw methods.

**Requirements:**
- Constructor takes account holder name and initial balance
- Implement deposit() method
- Implement withdraw() method (with overdraft protection)
- Implement `__str__()` for account info
- Add a property to get current balance

**Example:**
```
account = BankAccount("Alice", 100)
account.deposit(50)
account.withdraw(30)
print(account.balance)  # 120
```",
    "check50_slug": "cs50/problems/2024/python/bank",
    "difficulty": "medium",
    "common_mistakes": [
      "Not validating transaction amounts",
      "Not preventing overdrafts appropriately",
      "Not using properties correctly"
    ]
  }
]
